channel.title: GitHub Enterprise Cloud on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in GitHub Enterprise Cloud on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality is now generally available for GitHub Enterprise Cloud and GitHub Team. The announcement says it is intended to help with the growing volume of code produced with AI assistance.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: REST API endpoints for Visual Studio Subscription management
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Cloud admins can now use REST API endpoints to manage Visual Studio Subscription (VSS) assignments programmatically. The new endpoint returns all VSS assignments for an enterprise, including assignment details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise managed-settings.json is generally available
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Cloud now supports AI standards configuration through a managed-settings.json file stored in a .github-private repository within a selected organization. This gives enterprises a centralized way to define governance and related settings.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions: Minimum version enforcement timeline for self-hosted runners
item.description:
GitHub Actions is resuming enforcement of minimum version requirements for self-hosted runners on github.com and GitHub Enterprise Cloud with Data Residency. The post says this is part of a broader effort to rebuild enforcement, but the excerpt does not include the specific minimum versions or timeline details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise Live Migrations is now in public preview
item.description:
Enterprise Live Migrations (ELM) is now in public preview. It lets enterprise administrators migrate repositories from GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) to GitHub Enterprise Cloud.

  • Source: GitHub
